nodes.name: FilterStateInit
nodes.description: Initializes the IIR/FIR filter by storing numerator (b) and denominator (a) coefficients and bootstrapping the delay-line state vector (zi) to zeros. reset() is an integral part of this initialization stage and can also be re-invoked to flush the delay line between signals.
nodes.type_signature: (b: array-like of numerator coefficients, a: array-like of denominator coefficients) -> tuple[normalized numerator coefficient array, normalized denominator coefficient array, initial delay-line state vector, shape (max(len(a),len(b))-1,)]
nodes.conceptual_summary: RecurrenceKernelStateBootstrap. Accepts two coefficient arrays defining the feedforward and feedback gains of a linear recurrence relation, normalizes them relative to a lead scalar, and allocates a zero-initialized memory buffer whose length equals one less than the maximum order of the recurrence. This buffer encodes the 'history' required by the recurrence at any future evaluation step, and can be flushed back to the zero state to sever dependency on prior inputs, effectively resetting the system to a memoryless condition.. Applications: Control Systems Engineering: Initializing the internal state registers of a discrete-time PID or state-space controller before a simulation episode begins, ensuring the integrator and differentiator accumulators carry no stale history across independent plant trials., Computational Neuroscience: Bootstrapping the synaptic weight-delay buffer of a leaky integrate-and-fire neuron model at the onset of a stimulus epoch, where the recurrence coefficients encode membrane time constants and the state vector holds the membrane potential history over the refractory window., Digital Communications / Signal Chain DSP: Preparing the tap-weight and feedback registers of a decision-feedback equalizer (DFE) or adaptive lattice predictor before processing a new transmission burst, with the reset mechanism providing clean separation between independent data frames., Geophysical Simulation: Initializing the recursive accumulator of an autoregressive earth-layer reflection model (e.g., used in seismic deconvolution), where feedforward and feedback coefficient arrays encode layer impedance contrasts and the state vector must be zeroed between independent shot gathers.

nodes.name: FilterStep
nodes.description: Applies the online IIR/FIR filter to an incoming signal chunk using the current delay-line state. Consumes zi as immutable state-in and produces a new zi as state-out, enabling stateless composition across successive calls for true online (sample-by-sample or block) filtering.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.type_desc: 1-D array of input samples for the current chunk

inputs.name: zi
inputs.constraints: immutable; produced by FilterStateInit or previous FilterStep

outputs.name: filtered_signal
outputs.type_desc: 1-D array of filtered output samples, same length as input signal

outputs.name: zi_out
outputs.type_desc: updated delay-line state vector (state_out) to be threaded into the next FilterStep call
outputs.constraints: same shape as zi_in; never mutates zi_in

nodes.type_signature: (signal: 1-D array of input samples for the current chunk, b: numerator coefficient array, a: denominator coefficient array, zi: delay-line state vector (state_in), shape (max(len(a),len(b))-1,)) -> tuple[1-D array of filtered output samples, same length as input signal, updated delay-line state vector (state_out) to be threaded into the next FilterStep call]
nodes.conceptual_summary: ImmutableStatefulRationalLinearSequenceTransform. Applies a causal, rational linear transform — defined by a numerator and denominator polynomial coefficient pair — to a finite chunk of a scalar sequence, consuming an immutable delay-line memory vector that encodes the system's dependence on past inputs and outputs. The transform advances the internal memory by exactly the length of the input chunk and emits an output sequence of identical length, with the updated memory vector returned as a new, independent value rather than a mutation. This enables pure-functional, chunk-by-chunk composition of an infinite streaming computation by threading state-out into state-in across successive invocations.. Applications: Control systems engineering: applying a discretized PID or lead-lag compensator transfer function to a real-time sensor measurement stream, where each control loop tick is one chunk and the delay-line state preserves integrator/differentiator memory across ticks, Econometrics / time-series analysis: implementing an ARMA(p, q) model step to recursively produce one-step-ahead conditional expectations from an incoming block of observations, threading the lag-vector state across rolling estimation windows, Computational neuroscience: simulating the dendritic low-pass integration of a synaptic conductance waveform in a leaky integrate-and-fire neuron, where each chunk is a simulation time-step block and the state vector encodes the membrane potential history, Geophysics / seismology: applying a causal Butterworth or Chebyshev bandpass transform to continuous seismometer recordings ingested in fixed-size telemetry packets, preserving phase continuity across packet boundaries via the immutable state thread
